sections.heading: Clinical Follow-Up
sections.content:
History and physical examination for symptom assessment at each visit.
Frequency is typically every 3-6 months for the first 2-3 years, then every 6-12 months thereafter, though specific intervals are not mandated in the source.

sections.heading: Imaging Strategy
sections.content:
Chest CT and CT or MRI of the abdomen and pelvis with contrast (unless contraindicated) as clinically indicated.
Typically performed every 3-6 months for the first 2-3 years, then annually if no evidence of recurrence.
Imaging should be repeated prior to adjuvant therapy to assess for early recurrence.

sections.heading: Laboratory Monitoring
sections.content:
CA 19-9 level measurement is a category 2B recommendation. It should be measured after neoadjuvant treatment, prior to surgery, following surgery immediately prior to adjuvant therapy, and for surveillance.
CA 19-9 elevation alone, without other evidence of recurrence, is not a clear indication for treatment.
Liver function tests should be monitored periodically.

sections.heading: Supportive Follow-Up
sections.content:
Nutritional assessment and support as needed.
Pain management and assessment.
Psychosocial screening and support.
Monitoring for and management of exocrine pancreatic insufficiency with enzyme replacement therapy.
Assessment for treatment-related long-term toxicities.

timeline.title: Perioperative period
timeline.body:
CA 19-9 level measurement is a category 2B recommendation. It should be measured after neoadjuvant treatment, prior to surgery, following surgery immediately prior to adjuvant therapy.
Imaging should be repeated prior to adjuvant therapy to assess for early recurrence.

timeline.title: First 2-3 years (every 3-6 months)
timeline.body:
History and physical examination for symptom assessment at each visit.
Frequency is typically every 3-6 months for the first 2-3 years.
Chest CT and CT or MRI of the abdomen and pelvis with contrast (unless contraindicated) as clinically indicated. Typically performed every 3-6 months for the first 2-3 years.
CA 19-9 level measurement is a category 2B recommendation. It should be measured for surveillance.
Liver function tests should be monitored periodically.
CA 19-9 elevation alone, without other evidence of recurrence, is not a clear indication for treatment.
Nutritional assessment and support as needed.
Pain management and assessment.
Psychosocial screening and support.
Monitoring for and management of exocrine pancreatic insufficiency with enzyme replacement therapy.
Assessment for treatment-related long-term toxicities.

timeline.title: After 2-3 years (every 6-12 months or annually)
timeline.body:
History and physical examination for symptom assessment at each visit.
Frequency is typically every 6-12 months thereafter.
Chest CT and CT or MRI of the abdomen and pelvis with contrast (unless contraindicated) as clinically indicated. Then annually if no evidence of recurrence.
CA 19-9 level measurement is a category 2B recommendation. It should be measured for surveillance.
Liver function tests should be monitored periodically.
CA 19-9 elevation alone, without other evidence of recurrence, is not a clear indication for treatment.
Nutritional assessment and support as needed.
Pain management and assessment.
Psychosocial screening and support.
Monitoring for and management of exocrine pancreatic insufficiency with enzyme replacement therapy.
Assessment for treatment-related long-term toxicities.
